rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports, and in implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Insert AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks following AADL component type or implementation structure.
generic_rules: Use visible component features and ports in AGREE contracts; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level annexes.
Ensure AGREE variable declarations match the actual AADL data types of ports and features.
Only include range assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or named constants are provided.
Do not encode AADL properties such as timing or resource constraints as AGREE expressions unless explicitly represented in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid AADL feature names and types.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must align with the actual data types of the ports they reference.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
